literal: falling-toward, taking-refuge, surrender; from pra- + √pad 'to fall, to go'
invariant_definition: Total surrender to the Lord, taking refuge with him as one's sole means and goal. *Prapatti* is principally a Viśiṣṭādvaita and broader Vaiṣṇava category; it does not have a developed counterpart in Advaita or Dvaita's analytic apparatus, though all schools acknowledge the practice. It is structured by six classical *aṅgas* (*ānukūlyasya saṃkalpaḥ*, *prātikūlyasya varjanam*, *rakṣiṣyatīti viśvāsaḥ*, *goptṛtva-varaṇam*, *ātma-nikṣepaḥ*, *kārpaṇyam*) — six commitments / dispositions that define the act. Schools that develop the doctrine diverge on whether *prapatti* is an *upāya* (means) parallel to *bhakti* or whether it is the only *upāya* with *bhakti* itself dispensable, and on whether the post-*prapatti* soul retains agency in further *sādhana* (*Vaḍagalai* vs. *Teṅgalai* dispute).

per_school.definition: *Prapatti* is one of two *upāyas* (the other being *bhakti-yoga*); it is independently efficacious for those incapable of full *bhakti-sādhana* but does not abolish the *prapanna*'s ongoing duty to perform enjoined *karma* and to cooperate with the Lord's grace. The *markaṭa-nyāya* (monkey-clinging-to-mother analogy) describes the *prapanna*'s active grasp.

*Prapatti* is the sole *upāya*; *bhakti* itself is regarded as needlessly arduous. Once surrendered, the *prapanna* is wholly receptive — *upeya* IS the *upāya* (*siddhopāya*), and the soul's continued effort is in fact an obstacle. The *mārjāra-nyāya* (kitten-carried-by-mother analogy) describes the *prapanna*'s pure passivity.

*Śaraṇāgati* is the entry-condition for *bhakti*-sādhana; the term is used but not as a developed alternative path to *bhakti* in the way Vaḍagalai/Teṅgalai dispute over it. Once surrendered, the soul takes up *vaidhī-bhakti* and ascends to *rāgānugā* and *prema*.

translator_note: 'Surrender' is the standard English gloss and is acceptable, but it carries military / capitulation connotations that misread the *bhakti*-affective texture. *Prapatti* is closer to a child's letting-go into a parent's care: total trust as positive act. Note: the word is mostly absent from Advaita and Dvaita technical apparatus; if you see it, you are almost certainly reading a Viśiṣṭādvaita or Vaiṣṇava text.
